patch.value: File-backed schema sections (types/statuses/fields/type_workflows) leak into settings.json on writeSettings

readSettings merges file-backed schema sections from schema/types.json, schema/statuses.json, schema/fields.json and schema/workflows.json into the resolved settings object (settings.item_types.definitions and settings.schema.statuses/fields/type_workflows). Because callers like `pm config set` read via that merged object and then call writeSettings(settings), serializeSettings persists the MERGED schema back into settings.json. After that, editing or DELETING a rule in the schema/*.json file is silently ignored, because the stale copy baked into settings.json remains active (settings.json + file merge keeps the stale settings entry).

Pre-existing data-integrity bug surfaced during PR #106 (Codex review). Affects all file-backed schema sections; extended to type_workflows by the per-type workflow feature. Without it, file-driven schema customization (the documented workflow) becomes unreliable after any settings write.
